title: Charmed Life - Chapter 23 (Fragment 2)
text:
If you enter Chrestomanci Castle from one of the side doors, you will be confronted by a choice of stairs, corridors, doors, and more stairs. There is soft green carpet everywhere, flanked by highly polished wooden floors. Corridors, doors, more stairs. It is — no other way to describe it — a maze. In fact, to all but the very longest-serving members of the household, navigating Chrestomanci Castle is a most confusing challenge!

Of course, there is yet another way to enter Chrestomanci Castle — by magic. You will probably land in the centre of the tiled pentagram in the main hall. It’s best to make sure that you are definitely invited if you plan to pay a visit in this way, as otherwise you are likely to be caught in a particularly uncomfortable security device.

It is impossible to count all the rooms in Chrestomanci Castle. There are hundreds and hundreds, and even the resident Chrestomanci is unlikely to know exactly how many or even to have visited them all. There are the sorts of rooms you would expect to find in a castle, such as a huge dining room, and Large and Small Drawing Rooms (both massive). There are more unusual rooms too, such as the magician’s workshop. There’s an enormous library, which boasts a copy of every book in the world — and many more from other worlds as well.

If guests come to stay, there are plenty of bedrooms to choose from, each with its own bathroom. Some of the more interesting bedrooms are in the older, turreted part of the castle. Here, the rooms are round and the walls are nearly three-feet thick. If you kneel on one of the deep window seats and look out, you will see a breathtaking view of the castle grounds, with its smooth green lawns, immaculate flower gardens, and splendid orchards. If you look carefully, you might even get a glimpse of Chrestomanci’s private, walled garden. But don’t try and go there without his permission — you will probably find yourself somewhere else altogether!

Chrestomanci himself tends to influence the atmosphere of the building. In Gabriel De Witt’s day, Chrestomanci Castle was very businesslike and efficient — definitely the headquarters of a powerful magic task force! Then, later, when Gabriel realised the value of identifying and harnessing talent at an early age, it was opened up as an academy for children with magical abilities.

When Christopher Chant became Chrestomanci, the everyday business of policing magic carried on, with the invaluable help of his wife, Millie (also a powerful enchantress). But the arrival of two magical children of their own (Roger and Julia) and the adoption of two more (Eric and Gwendolen) meant that part of the castle was transformed into a family home.

The thing that everyone notices when they go to Chrestomanci Castle is the calm, quiet atmosphere everywhere, as if it were cloaked in a blanket of cotton wool. This is, of course, magic, for the whole building is suffused in it. Visitors who are up to no good feel very uncomfortable as the castle works on them, but if you live there for a while you gradually absorb what you need to help you deal with your own sort of magic.

When it’s time to leave Chrestomanci Castle, you’ll probably be quite sad to go. But a word of warning — don’t try to take any souvenirs with you unless they have been specifically given to you as gifts as they tend to protest in VERY LOUD VOICES !

vocabulary.definition: to take in and make part of something
vocabulary.example: If you live there for a while you gradually absorb what you need to help you with magic.

vocabulary.definition: a place of study or training in a special field
vocabulary.example: It was opened up as an academy for children with magical abilities.

vocabulary.definition: the act of legally taking another's child into one's family
vocabulary.example: The adoption of two more children meant part of the castle was transformed into a family home.

vocabulary.definition: the mood or feeling in a place
vocabulary.example: The thing that everyone notices is the calm, quiet atmosphere everywhere.

vocabulary.definition: to possess something to be proud of
vocabulary.example: There’s an enormous library, which boasts a copy of every book in the world.

vocabulary.definition: extremely impressive or beautiful
vocabulary.example: You will see a breathtaking view of the castle grounds.

vocabulary.definition: efficient and practical; serious
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci Castle was very businesslike and efficient.

vocabulary.definition: covered or concealed as if with a cloak
vocabulary.example: The castle is cloaked in a blanket of cotton wool.

vocabulary.definition: to face up to and deal with a problem or difficult situation
vocabulary.example: If you enter Chrestomanci Castle from one of the side doors, you will be confronted by a choice of stairs, corridors, doors, and more stairs.

vocabulary.definition: working well without wasting time or resources
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci Castle was very businesslike and efficient.

vocabulary.definition: a woman who uses magic or sorcery
vocabulary.example: Millie, also a powerful enchantress.

vocabulary.definition: very large in size or quantity
vocabulary.example: There’s an enormous library, which boasts a copy of every book in the world.

vocabulary.definition: to control and make use of natural resources or talents
vocabulary.example: Gabriel realised the value of identifying and harnessing talent at an early age.

vocabulary.definition: the main office of an organisation
vocabulary.example: Definitely the headquarters of a powerful magic task force!

vocabulary.definition: perfectly clean and tidy
vocabulary.example: Immaculate flower gardens.

vocabulary.definition: not able to be done or achieved
vocabulary.example: It is impossible to count all the rooms in Chrestomanci Castle.

vocabulary.definition: to have an effect on something or someone
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci himself tends to influence the atmosphere of the building.

vocabulary.definition: a complicated network of paths or passages, difficult to find one's way through
vocabulary.example: It is a maze.

vocabulary.definition: to find your way through a complicated place or situation
vocabulary.example: Navigating Chrestomanci Castle is a most confusing challenge!

vocabulary.definition: a five-pointed star, often a symbol used in magic
vocabulary.example: You will probably land in the centre of the tiled pentagram in the main hall.

vocabulary.definition: official agreement to do something
vocabulary.example: Don’t try and go there without his permission.

vocabulary.definition: the activity of maintaining order and following rules
vocabulary.example: The everyday business of policing magic carried on.

vocabulary.definition: an object kept as a reminder of a place or event
vocabulary.example: Don’t try to take any souvenirs with you unless they are gifts.

vocabulary.definition: magnificent and very impressive
vocabulary.example: Splendid orchards.

vocabulary.definition: spread through or over something
vocabulary.example: The whole building is suffused in magic.

vocabulary.definition: having small towers on the roof or walls, as in a castle
vocabulary.example: Some of the more interesting bedrooms are in the older, turreted part of the castle.
